reasoning: The informal statement is the classical consequence of the permutation action on left cosets: if H has finite index n, there exists a normal K ≤ H with [G:K] ≤ n!. In Mathlib, `Subgroup.index` returns 0 when the index is infinite. So `hH : H.index = n` together with `hn : n > 0` correctly encodes "H has finite index n" (n > 0 is already implicit in informal usage since the index is at least 1 whenever it is finite). The conclusion `∃ K ≤ H, K.Normal ∧ 0 < K.index ∧ K.index ≤ n.factorial` asks for a normal K ≤ H with finite index bounded by n!. The `0 < K.index` clause is necessary to correctly encode that K has finite index (otherwise K.index = 0 would trivially satisfy `≤ n.factorial` but represent infinite index — not matching the informal meaning |G:K| ≤ n!). This is exactly the implicit premise surfacing described in the guidelines, so the formalization is faithful.
